reasoning: The question asks whether ON RRP ONTTLD balances will exceed their April 13 baseline on four future dates. The baseline from trading on April 11 is $98.5B, which is an extreme local minimum—balances have routinely averaged well over $150B recently. For the nearest horizon (April 20), there is a strong probability (~74% based on FRED statistical momentum) of upward mean reversion driving the value above this low floor. However, a sweeping secular downward trend acts against this: the Fed's quantitative tightening (QT) has caused ON RRP balances to steadily drain toward zero. As QT continues into mid-2025, the expected baseline drops further, steadily eroding the chance of surpassing $98.5B. By July and October, macro uncertainties (e.g. when QT officially ends and whether the RRP facility balances bottom out at zero permanently) dominate, drawing probability estimates strictly back toward maximum uncertainty (50%).
